resources.string: Model is ready. Chat UI will be available here.
To use offline AI chat, download the on-device language model. The download may take several minutes depending on your connection.
Private to this app. Removed when the app is uninstalled. No extra permissions required.
Stored in this app\'s folder on shared storage. Removed when the app is uninstalled. Good for large files.
Saved under Downloads and kept after uninstall. Requires “All files access” in system Settings.
You can leave this screen; progress also appears in the notification.
Cannot start download. Notification permission is required to show download progress.
Cannot start download. All files access is required to save the model in Public storage.
The AI model is already saved in your public Downloads folder. Grant “All files access” so the app can load it, or cancel and download again into app storage.
All files access is required to use the model in Public storage. You can download the model into app internal storage instead.
Optional system instruction for the model…

Chooses which hardware runs the model.

• CPU — most compatible, usually slower.
• GPU — typically faster on phones with a capable GPU.
• NPU / TPU — specialized AI hardware when the device and model support it; may ignore sampling knobs like TopK, TopP, and Temperature.

Changing this reloads the model.

Maximum context length (in tokens) the model can keep in memory for the conversation.\n\nHigher values let the model remember longer chats, but use more RAM and can slow responses. If the chat grows past this limit, older turns may be dropped.
TopK limits each next-word choice to the K most likely tokens.\n\nLower TopK makes replies more focused and predictable. Higher TopK allows more variety. On NPU/TPU this setting may be ignored.

TopP (nucleus sampling) keeps the smallest set of tokens whose combined probability is at least P.\n\nLower TopP (closer to 0) is more conservative. Higher TopP (closer to 1) is more open-ended. On NPU/TPU this setting may be ignored.

Temperature controls randomness in generation.

Near 0: more deterministic, factual-sounding answers.
Near 1: balanced creativity.
Above 1: more varied or surprising replies, sometimes less coherent.

On NPU/TPU this setting may be ignored.

A system instruction steers the model\'s overall behavior for the whole chat (tone, language, role, or rules).\n\nExample: ask it to answer only in Persian, act as a tutor, or stay concise. Leave empty for the model\'s default behavior. Applying a new prompt reloads or resets the conversation session.
Ask the on-device model a question.
